message:
I've spent *years* being the friend who shows up to road trips without snacks, forgets her phone charger, and absolutely panics when the power goes out. But last month when my sister called in a minor home flooding situation, I actually had it together—extra blankets, a working flashlight, important documents in one spot, even a little emergency kit I'd finally put together.

She was so surprised. I was surprised at myself, honestly.

It's kind of wild how much better you *feel* when you're not scrambling. Not in a paranoid way—just in that "okay, I can handle this" way. I finally get why my mom was always talking about this stuff.

Have you had a moment where being a little prepared actually paid off in a real way?
